summary: Ukrainian FPV drone operators are conducting ambushes and strikes on Russian forces, including attacks on a Ukrainian BUK air defense system near Bolshoy Sokolny Island and targeting Russian troops on the Pokrovsk axis. Russian forces are adapting by using log-periodic directional antennas on "Molniyas" to improve drone range and resistance to jamming, while also employing tactics like charging at drones with bayonets. Meanwhile, NATO is testing drones and counter-drone systems at a new innovation range in Latvia, and Ukraine is collaborating with Britain to develop advanced maritime drones capable of open-ocean operations. Iranian-backed militias have also used fiber-optic FPV drones in an attack on a US base in Iraq.
